text: <par>Note the similarity between this form and <latinform>ngi̯ụ²</latinform> ‘body’ .</par>
<par>M1: rel. to 1390? M2: <emph>wo</emph>: cf. <latinform>ùl waʔ</latinform> above?</par>
<par>Note the similarity between this form and <latinform>ngɪ̯ə̣²</latinform> ‘back’ .</par>
<par>The first element <latinform>l-</latinform> 'penis' appears in reduced form in this "crypto-compound".</par>
<par>According to M. V. Sofronov 1978 there is some doubt as to the exact meaning of the Tangut graph he reconstructs here.</par>

<par>The immediate precursor of this form seems to have been something like <reconstruction>*m-tswa</reconstruction>. This seems to be a legitimate doublet of <latinform>lhi²</latinform>, from <reconstruction>*s-la</reconstruction>. These two words are written with distinct characters (Matisoff 1985:436).</par>
